message:
fix(gui,mux): guard against division-by-zero and underflow in rendering and resize

Harden arithmetic in several code paths that can receive zero-valued
terminal dimensions during early initialization, tmux attach, or
degenerate resize events:

- glyphcache.rs: use saturating_sub(1) for curly underline max_y and
  .max(1.0) for x_factor divisor to prevent divide-by-zero on
  zero-width cells
- render/pane.rs: replace subtraction chain in scrollbar thumb
  X-position with saturating_sub() to prevent underflow when
  padding + border exceeds pixel width
- resize.rs: clamp cell_h and cell_w with .max(1) before dividing
  avail_height and avail_width
- tab.rs: guard pixel_width/cols and pixel_height/rows divisions
  with if cols > 0 / if rows > 0 checks in SplitDirectionAndSize
  and cell_dimensions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
